title: anthosachne multiflora subsp. kingiana
intro: Anthosachne multiflora subsp. kingiana, also known as King's Wallaby Grass, is a rare species of grass native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antifungal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for medicinal use.
